title: zea oryzoides
intro: Zea oryzoides, also known as Japanese millet or barnyard millet, is a type of annual grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting kidney function. Zea oryzoides can be prepared as an herbal tea, decoction, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
